stories.title: With strikes above and crackdowns on the ground, Iranians describe life under siege
stories.description: A state-imposed internet blackout has obscured the reality of life in Iran as the war rages on. Those fleeing through neighboring countries share a rare glimpse of what life is like in Iran.

stories.title: Author Scott Anderson on Iran's power structure after death of top security official
stories.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with journalist and author Scott Anderson about Iran's power structure after the killing of Ali Larijani, the head of Iran's Supreme National Security Council.

stories.title: Iran confirms the death of its intelligence chief, 3rd top official killed in 24 hours
stories.description: Israel killed Iran's intelligence minister, Esmail Khatib, in an overnight strike — the third high-ranking official killed in about 24 hours.

stories.title: Cheap drones are reshaping modern warfare — and catching the U.S. off guard
stories.description: As Operation Epic Fury enters its third week, relentless attacks by cheap Iranian drones are being fended off by multi-million-dollar U.S. interceptors. How long can the math hold up?

stories.title: Morning news brief
stories.description: Iranians fleeing their country share rare glimpse of what life is like amid war, Trump floats idea of "taking" Cuba, the Federal Reserve is expected to hold its benchmark interest rate steady.

stories.title: Once the airstrikes stop, Iran's nuclear threat leaves no easy endgame
stories.description: Even after airstrikes end, Iran's nuclear threat looms and diplomacy may be too late.

stories.title: War with Iran strands about 20,000 seafarers in the Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Jacqueline Smith of the International Transport Workers' Federation about the roughly 20,000 seafarers stuck in the Strait of Hormuz in the midst of the Iran war.

stories.title: Surge in US gas prices deepens political peril for Trump over Iran
stories.description: The odds of a quick resolution to the war appear to be dwindling by the day, and the political stakes for the president are only growing.

stories.title: Wary allies show there's no quick fix to Trump's Iran crisis
stories.description: European leaders are hesitant to help Trump secure the Strait of Hormuz, but they know inaction on the Iran war is not really an option.

stories.title: Israel says 'limited' ground operations under way in Lebanon
stories.description: Troops are targeting "Hezbollah strongholds" in south Lebanon, where strikes reportedly killed nine people on Monday.

stories.title: How might the UK support shipping in the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: Ben Chu from BBC Verify has been looking at what role the Royal Navy could play in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Two charged in death of anti-Iranian regime activist in Canada
stories.description: Police say the motive remains under investigation but noted the three had disputes on social media.

stories.title: Who wants what from the Iran war?
stories.description: Most people, although not everybody, want this war to end as quickly as possible. But on what terms?

stories.title: Iran taking steps to prevent anti-establishment protests, Tehran residents tell BBC
stories.description: Residents describe new security checkpoints around the capital, where they say people are stopped and searched.

stories.title: Iran hits key UAE oil port and Dubai airport
stories.description: The port of Fujairah plays a crucial role in helping keep global supplies moving when the Strait of Hormuz is blocked.

stories.title: What Iranians are being told about the war
stories.description: Iranian state media's coverage of the war includes inflations of enemy casualties and digital manipulation intended to glorify Iran.

stories.title: Iranian footballer says 'everything will be fine' as she trains with Australian team
stories.description: Several players were granted Australian visas after concerns the team would face repercussions at home.

stories.title: Iran captain latest footballer to drop asylum bid, Iranian state media says
stories.description: Five of the seven members of the women's football delegation who originally sought to stay in Australia have now chosen to return home.

stories.title: US names six crew killed in refuelling plane crash in Iraq
stories.description: The aircraft was on a combat mission as part of ongoing US operations against Iran when it crashed in western Iraq on Thursday.

stories.title: BBC visits aftermath of Israeli strike on Lebanon that killed family as IDF targets Hezbollah
stories.description: A relative tells BBC those killed were civilians and not Hezbollah operatives, but the Israel Defense Forces says it was targeting "terrorist infrastructure".

stories.title: Why has the US targeted Iran's Kharg Island?
stories.description: The tiny island is home to one of the most critical pieces of Iran's energy infrastructure.

stories.title: Hamas urges key ally Iran to halt attacks on Gulf states
stories.description: The Palestinian group also affirms Tehran's right to defend itself from "aggression" by the US and Israel.

stories.title: 'Decapitation' strategy: Israel seeks to destroy Iran command and control
stories.description: Israel said on Wednesday its forces had killed another top Iranian official, Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib, and said its military was authorised to kill any senior figure of the Islamic republic in its sights. The announcement, the day after Iranian security chief Ali Larij...

stories.title: South Lebanon residents flee death and destruction
stories.description: Residents of Lebanon southern areas bombarded in the Israel-Hezbollah war poured into the ancient city of Sidon in search of safety. At war with Iran-backed Hezbollah, Israel has called on everyone living south of the Zahrani River to evacuate immediately. FRANCE 24's Claire Pacc...

stories.title: Trump pressures media on Iran war with claims of 'treason'
stories.description: US President Donald Trump and his allies are attacking news organisations for their coverage of the war on Iran, a conflict the administration says is going well for the United States but is unpopular with the public and has plunged the Middle East into chaos. Trump accused unspe...

stories.title: Tens of millions more in acute hunger if Mideast war drags on, UN warns
stories.description: An extra 45 million people could face acute hunger if the Mideast war carried on through June, swelling the number worldwide to a "terrible" high, the United Nations warned Tuesday. The war, now in its third week, has killed hundreds and seen Iran launch retaliatory strikes again...

stories.title: REPLAY: NATO chief Mark Rutte holds press conference
stories.description: NATO chief Mark Rutte said Wednesday that the military alliance's members were discussing the "best way" to open the Strait of Hormuz, through which a large chunk of the world's oil supply normally passes. "I have been in contact with many allies. We all agree, of course, that st...

stories.title: Zelensky on European tour to urge allies not to forget Ukraine
stories.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Wednesday was making his visit to Spain after a stop in London on Tuesday seen as part of an effort by Kyiv to keep the war against Russia a priority for European capitals, even as they grapple with fall-out from the US-Israeli war agains...

stories.title: Syrian refugees flee Israeli offensive in Lebanon, returning to a Syria in ruins
stories.description: At the Joussieh border crossing near the Syrian city of Homs, thousands of Syrian refugees are fleeing the devastating war in Lebanon between Israel and Hezbollah. Driven out by Israeli air strikes after years of exile in Lebanon, families and children wait for hours before retur...

stories.title: 'Sex and the City' in Paris: Candace Bushnell on her upcoming one-woman show
stories.description: In this edition of Entre Nous we're pleased to speak to Candace Bushnell, creator of "Sex and the City", the book that was the basis for the hit HBO show about four New York friends – Carrie, Miranda, Charlotte and Samantha – living and dating in the Big Apple. Candace has a one-...

stories.title: What to know about the resignation of Joe Kent, counterterrorism chief and Trump loyalist
stories.description: The head of the US's National Counterterrorism Center, Joe Kent, resigned from his post on Tuesday in opposition to the Trump administration's war on Iran. The former staunch Trump supporter said he could not support the war as "Iran posed no imminent threat" to the US.

stories.title: Scientists discover entire ecosystem hidden under ice of Antarctica
stories.description: An Antarctic mission is shining a light into the depths of the waters of the South Pole. Conservation group Under the Pole have been conducting marine research and raising awareness for almost 20 years. They've recently made some stunning discoveries by diving deep under the ice....

stories.title: To return to Gaza, Palestinian refugees first have to face Israeli-backed militias
stories.description: Israel has said it is reopening the Rafah border crossing between the Gaza Strip and Egypt on Wednesday. Israeli authorities had partially opened the crossing on February 2 as part of the peace plan agreed with Hamas in October, but closed it on February 28 when Israeli and US mi...

stories.title: F1, tennis... How the Middle East crisis has affected sporting events and athletes
stories.description: Formula One's Bahrain ​and Saudi Arabian Grands Prix will not take place in April due to the conflict in the Middle East, the sport announced on Saturday. The US and Israel's conflict with Iran has led ⁠to sporting events being ​postponed in the region, while competitions elsewhe...

stories.title: Iran firing banned cluster bombs at Israel
stories.description: Iran targeted Tel Aviv with missiles carrying cluster warheads in what it said was retaliation for Israel's assassination of Iran's security chief Ali Larijani, Iranian state television reported ​on Wednesday. The attack on densely populated Tel Aviv overnight on Tuesday killed t...

stories.title: How Los Angeles’s Iranian diaspora is confronting the US war on Iran
stories.description: Residents are divided between supporting and opposing the US and Israeli war against Iran, amid fears for loved ones.

stories.title: Iran threatens to strike Gulf energy facilities after South Pars attack
stories.description: Qatar foreign ministry spokesman slams Israeli attack on Iran's offshore gasfield as 'dangerous, irresponsible step'.

stories.title: How the killing of Iran’s Ali Larijani could impact the war
stories.description: How will the killing of Ali Larijani impact Iran’s leadership and the country’s security?

stories.title: How Dubai and UAE are being affected by Iran attacks and US-Israeli war
stories.description: How Dubai and UAE are being affected by Iran attacks and US-Israeli war

stories.title: Israel says it killed Iranian intelligence chief Khatib
stories.description: Israel’s Defence Minister Israel Katz said an overnight strike killed Iran’s Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib.

stories.title: Iran announces arrests, says US and Israel suffering ‘defeats’
stories.description: Hundreds have been arrested as Iran cracks down on 'traitors' following killing of Larijani, Soleimani.

stories.title: Iran holds funerals for officials Larijani and Soleimani
stories.description: Iranians have attended funeral processions for senior security officials Ali Larijani and Gholamreza Soleimani.

stories.title: Why did Trump’s counterterrorism chief resign over Iran war?
stories.description: US counterterrorism chief Joe Kent has resigned over Washington’s war on Iran, saying he cannot support the conflict.

stories.title: Not just energy: How the Iran war could trigger a global food crisis
stories.description: A critical fertiliser shortage threatens global food security amid Iran war.

stories.title: Iranian women footballers arrive in eastern Turkiye, on home border
stories.description: The team is on last leg of its journey home from Australia after Asian Cup, which began before conflict in Middle East.

stories.title: How Iran defied Trump threats to emerge as Strait of Hormuz gatekeeper
stories.description: Trump has called on other navies to help open the strait, but countries are instead striking deals with Iran.

stories.title: Iran confirms killing of intel minister in third assassination in two days
stories.description: Israel gives military permission to strike Iranian leaders at will as Iran confirms death of Esmaeil Khatib.

stories.title: Iranian diaspora celebrates Persian festival wrapped in US, Israeli flags
stories.description: Dancing on a beach in Barcelona, members of the Iranian diaspora celebrated the Persian festival of Chaharshanbe Suri.

stories.title: Conflict around Iran to push back global trade system — official
stories.description: Nikolay Patrushev emphasized that the Strait of Hormuz had been a connecting link for international logistical chains for years

stories.title: Middle East conflict spills beyond Persian Gulf borders — Russian Maritime Board chief
stories.description: "One of the striking examples is a US submarine torpedo attack on an Iranian frigate in the Indian Ocean," Nikolay Patrushev said

stories.title: US does not rule out attempts to seize nuclear fuel from Iran — White House
stories.description: A senior US administration official stated earlier that Washington had no intention of conducting any military operations targeting nuclear facilities in Iran at present

stories.title: West targeting Iran at OPCW — Russian permanent envoy
stories.description: Vladimir Tarabrin warned that any other countries that, for political, economic, or other reasons, "do not suit the Euro-Atlanticists" could be "next in line"

stories.title: US strikes more than 7,800 targets in Iran — White House
stories.description: White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt specified that the US military had sunk more than 120 Iranian ships

stories.title: Israeli Air Force delivers strike on major Iranian gas processing facility — TV
stories.description: According to the television broadcaster, this facility accounts for some 40% of Iran's overall gas production

stories.title: Losses due to situation with Iran may be up to 2% of global GDP — Russian Foreign Ministry
stories.description: "An increase in costs of baseline foods can be observed as early as in the near time due to the increase of energy prices, more expensive logistics and contraction of sowing areas," Maria Zakharova noted

stories.title: Ecological damage from strikes on Iran to engulf entire region — Russian MFA
stories.description: In Iran, strikes are targeting oil storage facilities that are located close to the country’s densely populated capital, Maria Zakharova pointed out

stories.title: Fire erupts at South Pars field in Iran after airstrikes
stories.description: Several development phases at the South Pars were put out of operation to prevent fire spread

stories.title: Russia to continue helping Iran — Foreign Ministry
stories.description: Maria Zakharova noted that Iran was facing terrible trials

stories.title: US officially states it has no information on Russia’s alleged aid to Iran — Kremlin
stories.description: Dmitry Peskov commented on a report by The Wall Street Journal that Moscow allegedly provided assistance to Tehran

stories.title: Israeli army says Iranian minister of intelligence was killed in targeted strike on Tehran
stories.description: The Israeli Air Force, acting on IDF intelligence, eliminated Esmaeil Khatib on Wednesday night
